trivia_bullets.text: The 'Alacrian' in the card name refers to Alacria, a plane connected to Avishkar by a relatively stable, predictable Omenpath that opens and closes on regular cycles. Alacrian Quickbeasts — the racing team represented by this card — are ancient, intelligent creatures fueled by aether that can sustain high speeds across varied terrain for days between feedings. The team is led by a rider-and-Quickbeast pair named Caradora and Lagorin, bound together by tragic circumstances back home [magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1; WebSearch snippet: Alacrian Quickbeasts MTG Wiki].
sources: magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1
WebSearch snippet: Alacrian Quickbeasts MTG Wiki Fandom

trivia_bullets.text: Alacrian Jaguar is a 4/4 Cat Mount (5 mana, Green) with Vigilance and the Saddle 1 mechanic: tap one or more other creatures you control with total power 1 or more to saddle it at sorcery speed. While saddled and attacking, it gets +2/+2 until end of turn (making it a 6/6 attacker). Scryfall confirms it is Pauper-legal — one of the few Mounts in Aetherdrift legal at that rarity floor. EDHREC rank 21,789. No flavor text [Scryfall: card 2bd40bca].

Art by Andrey Kuzinskiy. The Saddle mechanic was first introduced in Outlaws of Thunder Junction (2024), making Aetherdrift its second outing. Key difference from Vehicles: Mounts are already creatures and attack unsaddled; Saddle activates at sorcery speed (unlike Crew, which works at instant speed). Alacrian Jaguar's Saddle 1 threshold is extremely low — nearly any creature qualifies — making it one of the most easily saddled Mounts in the set [Scryfall: card 2bd40bca; magic.wizards.com: Aetherdrift Mechanics].
